source_title: DOW-UAP-D10, Mission Report, Middle East, May 2022
description_blurb:
This document is a Mission Report (MISREP), a standardized reporting form the U.S. Military uses to record the circumstances surrounding its operations. U.S. military services often use MISREPs to report Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) to AARO. The GENTEXT, or “general text” section of these reports often contains important qualitative, contextual information, distinguishing it from the more quantitative, or numerical, data found elsewhere in the report. 
A U.S. military operator reported observing “5x UAP fly across the screen.” The report continues by describing one of those observations as a “possible missile” and the remaining four as “possible birds.”
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D10, Mission Report, Middle East, May 2022
summary: A U.S. military operator, during an ISR mission in Iraq, observed five Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) via Full Motion Video (FMV) sensors. One UAP was described as having the visual reconnaissance profile of a possible missile, while the remaining four UAPs fit the profile of possible birds. Dust hindered ground collection during the mission.
reported_object_description: Five UAPs; one described as a "possible missile", four described as "possible birds"
reported_behavior: Fly across the screen; fly across the FOV
official_resolution: The report identifies five observations as UAPs, with the operator subjectively interpreting one as a possible missile and four as possible birds. The report notes that these characterizations are subjective and not conclusive.
bust_reasoning: The bust score is high because the document itself provides mundane explanations for the majority of the observed UAPs. Four out of five UAPs are explicitly described as 'possible birds', and the fifth as a 'possible missile'. The mention of 'dust hindered' FMV collection further supports the likelihood of misidentification due to environmental factors.

# DOW-UAP-D10, Mission Report, Middle East, May 2022 - Page 1

Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison
USCENTCOM Chief of Staff
Declassified on: 7 October 2025

SECRET//REL TO USA, FVEY

# Misrep undefined-7473483

## Narrative

(SECRET//REL TO USA, FVEY) AT 0246Z, [1.4a] TOOK OFF FROM [1.4a]
AT 0253Z, [1.4a] HANDED OVER WITH THE LRE WITH FULLY MISSION CAPABLE SENSORS.
AT 0958Z, [1.4a] BEGAN SIGINT COLLECTION.

FROM 0958Z TO 2036Z, [1.4a] CONDUCTED TARGET DEVELOPMENT ON OP [1.4a]
ISO [1.4a] SEE ISR 1).
AT 1514Z, [1.4a] OBSERVED 1X UAP (SEE OBSERVATION 1).

AT 2036Z, [redacted] RETURNED TO BASE.
AT 2036Z, [1.4a] STOPPED SIGINT COLLECTION.
AT 2257Z, [redacted] HANDED BACK TO THE LRE.

FULL MOTION VIDEO WAS EXPLOITED BY DGS1.

AT 0004Z, [1.4a] LANDED AT [1.4a] WITH A LAST ENGINE SHUT-DOWN TIME OF 0014Z.

[1.4a] [1.4a] 1 IMINT TASKING PROSECUTED, [1.4a] HOURS, 1 SIGINT TASKING PROSECUTED, 2 TOTAL TASKINGS PROSECUTED; 1 OBSERVATION

(S/RELIDO) Mission Narrative

## Admin

### CLASSIFICATION
* Classification: SECRET
* Associated Caveats: REL TO USA, FVEY
* Classification Source (MS or Classification Guide ID): MS
* Declassification Date (YYYYMMDD): 20470506

## OPERATION

| (b)(6) |
| :--- |

SECRET//REL TO USA, FVEY

cover_up_reasoning: The document contains routine redactions of names, unit identifiers, and specific locations, consistent with standard operational security and FOIA practices. The classification as 'SECRET' and its declassification date are also routine. The explicit 'Approved for Release to AARO' indicates a process of disclosure rather than active concealment of anomalous information.
summary_zh: 一名美国军事操作员在伊拉克执行ISR任务期间，通过FMV传感器观察到五个不明异常现象 (UAP)。其中一个UAP被描述为具有“可能导弹”的视觉侦察特征，而其余四个UAP符合“可能鸟类”的特征。任务期间，沙尘阻碍了地面信息收集。
reported_object_description_zh: 五个UAP；其中一个被描述为“可能导弹”，四个被描述为“可能鸟类”
official_resolution_zh: 该报告将五次观察识别为UAP，操作员主观地将其中一个解释为可能导弹，将四个解释为可能鸟类。报告指出，这些特征描述是主观的，并非结论性的。
bust_reasoning_zh: 驳斥得分较高，因为文件本身对大多数观察到的UAP提供了世俗解释。五个UAP中有四个被明确描述为“可能鸟类”，第五个被描述为“可能导弹”。文件中提及“沙尘阻碍”FMV收集，进一步支持了因环境因素导致误判的可能性。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件包含对姓名、单位标识符和具体地点的常规编辑，这符合标准的操作安全和《信息自由法》(FOIA) 惯例。其“秘密”分类及其解密日期也属常规。明确注明“批准向AARO发布”表明这是一个披露过程，而非主动隐瞒异常信息。
claims: One UAP had the visual profile of a possible missile
Four UAPs had the visual profile of possible birds
claims_zh: 一个UAP具有“可能导弹”的视觉特征
四个UAP具有“可能鸟类”的视觉特征
bust_explanations: Bird, bat, or insect at close range: The report explicitly states that four of the five observed UAPs 'fit closer to the profile of possible birds'.
Misidentified known aircraft/object: The first UAP was described as a 'possible missile', indicating a known object type, even if its identification was uncertain.

Weather/atmospheric phenomenon: The document notes that 'DUST HINDERED MOST FMV COLLECTION OF THE GROUND', suggesting environmental conditions that could contribute to misidentification or obscure details of objects in the field of view.
bust_explanations_zh: 近距离的鸟类、蝙蝠或昆虫：报告明确指出，观察到的五个UAP中有四个“更符合可能鸟类的特征”。
误认的已知飞机/物体：第一个UAP被描述为“可能导弹”，表明其属于已知物体类型，即使其识别不确定。
天气/大气现象：文件指出“沙尘阻碍了大部分地面FMV收集”，表明环境条件可能导致误判或模糊视场内物体的细节。
cover_up_indicators: Redaction of unit identifiers and specific locations ('[1.4a]', '[1.4g]')
cover_up_indicators_zh: 对姓名/代号的常规编辑（例如，“(b)(3), 130b, (b)(6)”，“[已编辑]”）
对单位标识符和具体地点的编辑（“[1.4a]”，“ [1.4g]”）
《信息自由法》编辑的标准化套话
